metadata.summary: Enterprise project management supporting four process templates with work-item hierarchy, Kanban boards, WIP limits, WIQL queries, and REST API for agent automation.

content.title: Azure DevOps Boards
content.summary: Microsoft's enterprise project management tool supporting four process templates (Basic, Agile, Scrum, CMMI) with a work-item hierarchy (Epic → Feature → User Story/PBI → Task), configurable Kanban boards, WIP limits, WIQL query language, and full REST API. Integrates natively with Azure Pipelines, Repos, and Test Plans. Agents operate via PAT-scoped REST API or az boards CLI; treat process-template definitions as YAML stored in version control.

why.p: Enterprise Microsoft-stack organisations need a single project management layer that integrates with Entra ID (SSO), Azure DevOps Pipelines (build traceability), and Power BI (Analytics OData). The CMMI template provides built-in Risk, Change Request, and Review work item types required for regulated industries. AB#<id> commit syntax links code changes to work items automatically.

list.item: Microsoft-ecosystem organisations requiring Entra ID SSO and tenant-level governance.
Regulated or audit-heavy projects needing CMMI work item types and audit trail.
Teams using Azure Pipelines/Repos/Test Plans — Boards integrates build, PR, and release traceability.
Portfolio reporting via Azure DevOps Analytics + Power BI.
Hybrid on-prem (Azure DevOps Server) requirements where SaaS Jira is not permitted.
Engineering-only teams preferring Linear or GitHub Projects — ADO Boards UI is heavier.
Teams primarily on GitHub — GitHub Projects v2 avoids identity and permission duplication.
Open-source or community projects — ADO licensing assumes commercial accounts.
Lightweight single-team Kanban — Trello, ClickUp, or Linear ship faster with less configuration.

section.name: setup-templates-hierarchy
section.title: ADO Boards Setup: Templates, Hierarchy, and Board Configuration

rule.statement: Pick one process template per organisation and inherit from it — never fork. Mass-edit migrations across forks are painful and irreversible.
rule.rationale: Custom-process forks diverge quickly; template inheritance lets you propagate org-wide field changes centrally.

list.item: Basic — Issue + Task: simple projects or teams new to ADO.
Agile — Epic, Feature, User Story, Task, Bug: standard agile teams.
Scrum — Epic, Feature, PBI, Task, Bug, Impediment: Scrum teams with formal impediment tracking.
CMMI — Epic, Feature, Requirement, Task, Bug, Change Request, Review, Risk: regulated/audit-heavy environments.

section.name: work-item-hierarchy
section.title: Work Item Hierarchy
section.p: Agile process hierarchy (top to bottom): Epic (initiative) → Feature (shippable capability) → User Story (user value) → Task (technical unit) → Bug (defect, any level).

rule.statement: Use Area Path for team ownership and Iteration Path for time. Do not overload either dimension with the other's concerns.
rule.rationale: Area/Iteration confusion is the most common ADO setup error: teams assign work to an iteration that belongs to a different area team, creating invisible cross-team dependencies.

rule.statement: Set WIP limits per board column and enforce them via card-style rules that colour blocked or over-limit columns red. Without enforced limits, the Active column becomes a backlog landfill.
rule.rationale: WIP limits are the primary Kanban control mechanism; visual enforcement makes violations visible in stand-up without anyone having to report them.

example.title: Board column configuration with WIP limits
example.code: columns: - name: "New" state_mapping: ["New"] wip_limit: null - name: "Active" state_mapping: ["Active"] wip_limit: 5 split: true # Doing / Done sub-columns - name: "Resolved" state_mapping: ["Resolved"] wip_limit: 3 - name: "Closed" state_mapping: ["Closed"] swimlanes: - name: "Expedite" - name: "Default" - name: "Tech Debt" card_styles: - name: "Blocked" condition: "Tags Contains 'Blocked'" background_color: "#FF0000"

rule.statement: Define Iteration paths with a Release/Sprint hierarchy before the first sprint starts; agents that create missing iterations in production break sprint history.
rule.rationale: Iteration path creation is additive — once a sprint exists, renaming or restructuring it invalidates all historical velocity data.

section.name: queries-automation-api
section.title: WIQL, Automation, REST API, and Agent Gotchas

example.title: My active items ordered by priority
example.code: SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title], [System.State] FROM WorkItems WHERE [System.AssignedTo] = @Me AND [System.State] IN ('New', 'Active') ORDER BY [Microsoft.VSTS.Common.Priority]

example.title: Current sprint burndown items
example.code: SELECT [System.Id], [System.Title], [System.State], [Microsoft.VSTS.Scheduling.RemainingWork] FROM WorkItems WHERE [System.IterationPath] = @CurrentIteration AND [System.WorkItemType] IN ('User Story', 'Bug', 'Task')

rule.statement: Save WIQL as Shared Queries with stable URLs; agents should reference the URL, not the query body, so query updates do not break consumers.
rule.rationale: Query body references in agent code create drift — when the query changes, every consumer breaks silently.

rule.statement: Note that @CurrentIteration is team-scoped; an agent querying across teams sees stale or wrong iteration data unless the team is explicitly specified.
rule.rationale: Multi-team ADO organisations have one iteration tree shared differently across team contexts. Unqualified @CurrentIteration returns the default team's iteration.

rule.statement: Use Boards Automation Rules for simple state transitions; use Azure Pipelines or Power Automate for cross-system workflows that exceed the Boards rule engine's capabilities.
rule.rationale: Boards automation rules are limited (no joins, no cross-project triggers); forcing complex logic into them produces fragile, invisible workflows.

rule.statement: Scope PAT tokens to the minimum required permissions per agent role; rotate every 90 days. Never store PAT in description fields — they are not encrypted beyond ADO defaults.
rule.rationale: PAT scope sprawl means a compromised token has organisation-wide write access. Description field storage creates a credential leak in work item history.

rule.statement: JSON Patch operations are order-sensitive in the ADO API; set required fields before setting State, or the operation returns 200 with a ValidationError in the body.
rule.rationale: Silent ValidationErrors in 200 responses are the most common ADO API integration bug — the caller assumes success but the item was not updated.

rule.statement: System.Tags is a semicolon-separated string, not an array; an agent setting it naively overwrites all existing tags.
rule.rationale: Tag overwrites are silent and not recoverable from standard history; read-then-merge is required for safe tag updates.

antipattern.description: Agents marking work items "Closed" without verifying acceptance evidence is attached.
antipattern.reason: Closing without evidence creates an audit gap. For regulated projects (CMMI), closure without an attached acceptance artefact fails SOX/SOC2 review.

antipattern.description: Linking by bare work item ID instead of full URL in relation payloads.
antipattern.reason: ADO silently drops bare-ID relations. The relation must reference the full work-item URL to be persisted.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.title: Agentic Workflow and Integration
section.p: A subagent operates against the ADO REST API (PAT or Microsoft Entra service principal), creating/updating work items, running WIQL queries, and updating board state. Use the az boards CLI for shell-friendly automation; use REST for batch operations and dashboards. Treat the process template as code: store custom fields/states/rules in YAML, apply via API, version in repo. Human-in-loop required for state-template changes, sprint commitments, and release approvals — agents may set "Active" but not "Closed" without verification.

list.item: faion-pm-agent — sprint planning, backlog refinement, capacity calc.
faion-sdd-executor-agent — converts SDD TASK_*.md → User Story/Task work items with wbs_id field.
faion-business-analyst — generates User Stories with Gherkin acceptance criteria.
faion-software-developer — links commits/PRs to work items via AB#<id> syntax.
az (Azure CLI) + az devops — Auth, projects, repos (https://learn.microsoft.com/cli/azure/devops).
az boards — Work item CRUD, queries (https://learn.microsoft.com/cli/azure/boards).
az pipelines — Trigger builds linked to work items (https://learn.microsoft.com/cli/azure/pipelines).
tfx-cli — Process template export/import, extension publish (https://github.com/microsoft/tfs-cli).
python-tfs / azure-devops Python SDK — Programmatic access (pip install azure-devops).
httpie — Quick REST API exploration with PAT (https://httpie.io).
Azure DevOps Services — SaaS, REST 7.x, GraphQL via Analytics; cloud, default.
Azure DevOps Server — On-prem, same APIs; self-hosted, regulated/airgapped orgs.
Azure DevOps Analytics — SaaS, OData v4; source for Power BI dashboards.
Power BI — SaaS, REST + Analytics; Burndown, velocity, CFD.
GitHub — SaaS, sync via "Boards-GitHub Integration"; mirror commits/PRs as work item links.
Marketplace extensions — SaaS, Mixed; vet for active maintenance.
Microsoft Teams — SaaS, connector + Graph API; notifications on board events.
Power Automate — SaaS, connector; cross-system automation if Boards rules aren't enough.
Pick one process template per org and inherit (don't fork): mass-edit migrations across forks are painful.
Set WIP limits per board column and surface them in card-style rules; without limits Boards become "Active" landfills.
Use Area Path for ownership, Iteration Path for time. Don't overload either.
Link commits/PRs with AB#<id> so traceability is automatic; don't rely on manual association.
Capacity planning: enter team member days off; velocity forecasts are only useful with capacity adjustment.
Save WIQL as Shared Queries with stable URLs; agents reference URL, not query body, so query updates don't break consumers.
For audit: enable Process History export; CMMI states (Proposed/Active/Resolved/Closed) plus the audit trail satisfy SOX/SOC2.
PAT scoping: read-only by default; write-scope tokens per agent role; rotate every 90 days.
Iteration paths must be created before assignment; agent that "fixes" a missing iteration by creating one in production breaks sprint history.
WIQL @CurrentIteration is team-scoped; agent across teams sees stale data unless team is specified.
System.Tags is a semicolon-separated string, not an array; agents naively setting it overwrite existing tags.
JSON Patch ops are order-sensitive; setting State before required fields fails silently with 200 + ValidationError in body.
Throttling: ADO returns 429 + Retry-After under load; agents must implement backoff (Microsoft documents an 18,750 TSTU/5min limit).
Linking by full URL is required for relations; bare IDs are silently dropped.
Process customisation via REST has eventual consistency: read-after-write may show stale schema for ~30s.
"Closed" state from agents requires verifying acceptance evidence is attached; otherwise audit gap.
Personally-Identifying-Information: Description fields are not encrypted at rest beyond ADO defaults; never store secrets there.
Azure Boards docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/
REST API reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/azure/devops/wit/
Process customisation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/organizations/settings/work/customize-process
WIQL syntax: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/queries/wiql-syntax
Analytics OData: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/report/extend-analytics/data-model-analytics-service
Rate limits: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/integrate/concepts/rate-limits
az devops CLI: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/devops
Python SDK: https://github.com/microsoft/azure-devops-python-api

template.purpose: Sprint planning document with capacity table, backlog, risks, DoD.
User Story with As-a/I-want/So-that and Gherkin acceptance criteria.
